# Configuration of Access Barriers

Similarly to the display panels, Spatium allows you to create access barriers to take actions when necessary.

To add a new access barrier, enter edit mode by clicking the “Spatium Toolbox” button to configure and modify access barriers in the relevant zone.

<figure><img src="../../.gitbook/assets/image (66).png" alt=""><figcaption></figcaption></figure>

You can add new access barriers by typing “Add a Barrier”. A floating menu will appear on the left side of the screen.

<figure><img src="../../.gitbook/assets/image (67).png" alt=""><figcaption></figcaption></figure>



The barrier will then appear in the center of the layout (as outlined in red in the image above), already selected, and a floating menu will appear on the left side of the screen to configure the following:

* Name – The name of the barrier will be displayed at the center of the barrier on the layout;
* Identifier.

**Note**: The unique identifier will be provided by the manufacturer.

It is also possible to represent the direction of the barrier, either entry or exit, by applying a rotation. The direction of the arrows will automatically adjust accordingly.

<figure><img src="../../.gitbook/assets/image (68).png" alt=""><figcaption></figcaption></figure>



Once the panel is configured, click “Apply”, otherwise the modification will not be saved, then click “Save” to exit edit mode.

<p align="center"><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt=""><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt=""></p>

Once your barriers are created, you can select them to perform actions such as manually opening them when necessary. To do this, simply select the barrier(s) in question and then press the “Open Barrier” button. A confirmation or error message will appear in the top right corner of the screen to confirm the operation.

<figure><img src="../../.gitbook/assets/image (69).png" alt=""><figcaption></figcaption></figure>
